article_meta.title: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY) called the SAVE America Act “one of the most despicable pieces of legislatio

reasoning: The model does a strong job identifying genuine bias in the author's own prose while correctly avoiding flagging quoted text from Schumer. All three flagged items are defensible instances of author-level bias. The 'dragging his feet' flag is clearly correct (Spin). The 'election integrity bill' flag is a good catch of Word Choice bias. The 'unwillingness' framing is a reasonable Slant detection. The model could arguably have also flagged 'Schumer continued his rant' as Spin/Word Choice (the word 'rant' is a loaded characterization by the author), and 'finally said' contains editorial judgment. These are minor misses but 'rant' in particular is a clear instance.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged items are from the author's own prose. No quoted text was flagged as bias. Perfect compliance with the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three items are defensible. 'Dragging his feet' is clearly loaded. 'Election integrity bill' adopts supporter framing. 'Unwillingness' framing is a reasonable catch, though slightly more debatable. Overall very solid.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed 'Schumer continued his rant' - where 'rant' is a clearly loaded word choice by the author characterizing Schumer's statements disparagingly. It also missed 'finally said' which contains editorial judgment implying Thune was unreasonably slow. These are notable omissions, particularly 'rant.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Spin for 'dragging his feet' is correct. Word Choice for 'election integrity bill' is correct. Slant for the Thune 'unwillingness' framing is defensible, though Word Choice or Spin might also apply. Overall the labels are well-applied.
